---
format: html
title: get_aux_classification_table()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>get_aux_classification_table</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Get auxiliary classification table</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Reads the manually curated auxiliary classification table from a
CSV file. If the file does not yet exist, returns an empty tibble
with the required columns so that the rest of the pipeline can
continue and detect any missing taxa.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>get_aux_classification_table(
  file_path = here::here("Data/Input/aux_classification_table.csv")
)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="file_path">file_path</code></td>
<td>
<p>A length-1 character string giving the path to the CSV file.
Defaults to
'here::here(&quot;Data/Input/aux_classification_table.csv&quot;)'.
The file, if present, must contain columns 'sel_name',
'family', 'genus', and 'species'.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Details</h3>

<p>Manual classifications in this file override automatic
classifications produced by 'get_taxa_classification()'. When
the file exists it is validated to confirm all required columns
are present. Missing columns are filled with 'NA_character_'
rather than raising an error, so that partial tables are
accepted.
</p>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>A tibble with columns 'sel_name' (character), 'family'
(character), 'genus' (character), and 'species' (character).
Returns an empty tibble when the file does not exist.
</p>


<h3>See Also</h3>

<p>[combine_classification_tables()],
[check_and_report_missing_taxa()]
</p>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
